ORA-19638.m: The checkpoint of the target for this incremental backup is less than the start of the incremental backup. If this backup were applied, then any changes made between the datafile checkpoint and the start of the incremental backup could be lost.

ORA-19639.m: The checkpoint of the target for this incremental backup is greater than or equal to the checkpoint of the file in the incremental backup set. This backup cannot advance the checkpoint of the target file, so there is no point in applying it.

ORA-19640.m: This message identifies the datafile checkpoint for a datafile that was too old to take an incremental backup from, or the target of an incremental restore that could not be applied.

ORA-19641.m: This message identifies the checkpoint of a datafile in an incremental backup set that could not be applied.

ORA-19642.m: This message identifies the starting SCN of an incremental backup that could not be applied.

ORA-19643.m: The incremental-start SCN which was specified when starting an incremental datafile backup is greater than the datafile checkpoint SCN, which could cause some blocks to be missed.

ORA-19644.m: The incremental-start SCN which was specified when starting an incremental datafile backup is less than the RESETLOGS SCN.

ORA-19645.m: The incremental-start SCN which was specified when starting an incremental datafile backup is less than the datafile's creation SCN.

ORA-19646.m: Restore needs to adjust the size of the file as indicated, but the file size adjustment failed.

ORA-19647.m: BACKUPSETDATAFILE was called with a non-zero backup_level and a FALSE incremental indication.

ORA-19648.m: The incremental-start SCN which was specified when starting an incremental datafile backup is equal to the datafile's checkpoint SCN.

ORA-19649.m: APPLYOFFLINERANGE was called with a RECID/STAMP which was not found in the indicated control file. This probably means that the specified control file is no longer the same control file that Recovery Manager thinks it is.

ORA-19650.m: This messages identifies the offline-clean SCN from the indicated offline- range record in the indicated file.

ORA-19651.m: APPLYOFFLINERANGE cannot apply an offline-range record to a target datafile unless the datafile's checkpoint SCN exactly matches the offline-clean SCN in the specified offline-range record.

ORA-19652.m: The target datafile for an APPLYOFFLINERANGE call is fuzzy.

ORA-19653.m: SWITCHTOCOPY was called with a datafile copy for a datafile that was dropped prior to the time this control file was backed up.

ORA-19654.m: This SWITCHTOCOPY operation is attempting to switch incarnations of a datafile, but the currently mounted control file is not a backup control file.

ORA-19655.m: This SWITCHTOCOPY operation is attempting to switch to a datafile which comes from a different RESETLOGS version of the database.

ORA-19656.m: The indicated log file is an active log. You can only backup, copy, or delete archived logs.

ORA-19657.m: The file being inspected is already part of the currently mounted database.

ORA-19658.m: The RESETLOGS data in the log file being inspected does not match that in the currently mounted control file.

ORA-19659.m: This incremental backup cannot be applied to the specified datafile, because the datafile is from an earlier incarnation of the database, and its checkpoint would be advanced too far to be recoverable in the current incarnation of the database.

ORA-19660.m: A restore conversation was made to verify all the files in a backup set, and the files which were printed in messages 19661 or 19662 could not be verified because corrupt blocks for those files were found in the backup set.

ORA-19661.m: Some data blocks for the indicated datafile were corrupt in the backup set.

ORA-19662.m: Some data blocks for the indicated archived log were corrupt in the backup set.

ORA-19663.m: An attempt was made to apply the current offline range to the specified datafile, but the datafile is either not current enough or is not at the correct SCN to apply the offline range.

ORA-19665.m: The size of the file as indicated in the file header does not match the true size of the file. The two differing sizes are shown in units of logical blocks.

ORA-19697.m: The standby control file could not be restored because it is not in this backup set.

ORA-19712.m: The table name string exceeds maximum length.

ORA-19713.m: The copy number is not in a valid range or you have reached maximum limit.

ORA-19718.m: The specified command id exceeds the maximum length for command id.

ORA-19719.m: The specified operation name exceeds the maximum length for operation name.

ORA-19720.m: This is most likely caused by an invalid SCN number that came from an external file, such as an export file.

ORA-19721.m: One of the data files that should have been in the pluggable set was not found. Alternatively, one of the data files in the pluggable set had already been plugged in from a previous import job and so was excluded from the pluggable set.

ORA-19722.m: The datafile is an incorrect version. It contains either less or more changes then the desired version.

ORA-19723.m: The data file was plugged in read only or plugged in as part of pluggable database creation or pluggable database plug-in. It cannot be re-created.

ORA-19724.m: The snapshot SCN is before the SCN at which the referred datafile is plugged into the database.

ORA-19725.m: There maybe another "ALTER DATABASE RESET COMPATIBILITY" command issued concurrently, preventing this process from acquiring the plug-in enqueue.

ORA-19726.m: Some of the data in the pluggable set requires a compatibility level higher than what is currently allowed by the database. The string in square bracket is the name of the compatibility type associated with the data.

ORA-19727.m: Some of the data in the pluggable set requires a compatibility level higher than the release level of the Oracle executable. The string in square bracket is the name of the compatibility type associated with the data. Chapter 83 83-24

ORA-19728.m: The non-partitioned table has the same data object number as one of the partitions in the partitioned table. One can not exchange the table with the partition in this case.

ORA-19729.m: The file is not the initial version of the plugged in datafile.

ORA-19732.m: The number of datafiles in the export file for the referred tablespace is not the same as expected. This is most likely caused by a user editing the export file.

ORA-19733.m: The COMPATIBLE initialization parameter is not high enough to allow the operation. Allowing the command would make the database incompatible with the release specified by the current COMPATIBLE parameter.

ORA-19734.m: When a tablespace is plugged into a database, the tablespace is initially read-only.

ORA-19735.m: When a tablespace is plugged into a database, the tablespace is initially read-only.

ORA-19738.m: The compatibility check failed because a character set name that was provided is not valid.

ORA-19765.m: The change tracking file does not match the one that was present when this instance was opened. In RAC, this error is most likely caused by having a change tracking file that cannot be consistently updated by all instances.

ORA-19768.m: The USING clause was specified with DISABLE CHANGE TRACKING

ORA-19770.m: The USING clause was specified with ALTER DATABASE ENABLE BLOCK CHANGE TRACKING, but no file name was given.

ORA-19781.m: The PROXY option was used with a sparse file. This is not supported.

ORA-19784.m: The change tracking writer could not get requested memory for buffer allocation.

ORA-19802.m: There are two possible cause for this error: 1) The DB_RECOVERY_FILE_DEST parameter was in use when no DB_RECOVERY_FILE_DEST_SIZE parameter was encountered while fetching initialization parameter.

ORA-19803.m: Parameter DB_RECOVERY_FILE_DEST_SIZE specified was not valid.

ORA-19804.m: Disk space of specified bytes from the DB_RECOVERY_FILE_DEST location for DB_RECOVERY_FILE_DEST_SIZE limit could not be reclaimed.

ORA-19805.m: The file described by the record in control file was deleted in order to reclaim disk space from recovery area for other operations or deleted by another RMAN session.

ORA-19806.m: Duplex backup to recovery area is not supported.

ORA-19807.m: The duplex number specified is outside of the expected range.

ORA-19808.m: The value of parameters DB_RECOVERY_FILE_DEST and DB_RECOVERY_FILE_DEST_SIZE must be same in all instances. instance. All databases must have same recovery destination parameters.

ORA-19812.m: There are three possible cause for this error: 1) The indicated parameter was in use when no DB_RECOVERY_FILE_DEST parameter was encountered while fetching the initialization parameter.

ORA-19813.m: An attempt was made to change a backup piece or image copy in recovery area to UNAVAILABLE.

ORA-19815.m: DB_RECOVERY_FILE_DEST is running out of disk space.

ORA-19816.m: One of the following events caused this: 1. A database crash happened during file creation. 2. A backup control file was restored. 3. The control file was re-created. 4. DB_RECOVERY_FILE_DEST has previously been enabled and then disabled.

ORA-19846.m: Unable to read the file header from remote site.

ORA-19849.m: This error should be followed by other errors indicating the cause of the problem.

ORA-28535.m: A Heterogeneous Services agent's driver module called an HS service routine with an invalid HS context value. This probably is a logic error in the driver.

ORA-28546.m: A failure occurred during initialization of a network connection from the Oracle server to a second process: The connection was completed but a disconnect occurred while trying to perform protocol-specific initialization, usually due to use of different network protocols by opposite sides of the connection.

ORA-37530.m: When aggregating a partitioned variable the dimensionality of any compressed partitions should be identical.

ORA-37531.m: When aggregation crosses partition boundaries within a compressed variable the individual partitions must all be aggregated using a similar aggmap over the same unaltered relations.

ORA-37541.m: When a low level hierarchy element can reach an aggregate element by more than one path through the hierarchy, it is said to have multiple paths. The danger is that the value of the low level hierarchy element will have its value counted multiple times (once per path) in the total of the aggregate element.

ORA-37542.m: A hierarchy is balanced if: 1) All members of a level are at the same depth from the root of the hierarchy and 2) All the members in different levels are at different depths from the root of the hierarchy.

ORA-37543.m: Two hierarchies are inconsistent when members that appear in both hierarchies have different ultimate leaves.

ORA-37544.m: A dimension is "star consistent" when it can be represented in a star schema table. This requires that a hierarchy level map to a single underlying column. If a level is shared across one or more hierarchies, it must have the same set of members in each hierarchy (to share a column).

ORA-37545.m: A hierarchy has loops when the ancestor of a dimension member uses that member as its parent. Note that some loops are created across hierarchies. For example, in hierarchy 1, dimension member A is the parent of B, B is the parent of C and in hierarchy 2, C is that parent of A forms a hierarchy loop.

ORA-37546.m: An empty levelorder valueset was specified for the HIERSHAPE function.

ORA-37547.m: DIMENSION_MEMBER did not have an associated level. Level based hierarchies must have a level for every dimension member.

ORA-37548.m: When a dimension member is part of a hierarchy, it appears in the INHIER valueset associated with the hierarchy. This dimension member is missing from this value set and thus should not be part of this hierarchy.

ORA-37549.m: A dimension member has a parent that is lower or on the same level as this member according to the levelorder valueset.

ORA-37550.m: A dimension member had a parent that was not one level above the level of this member according to the LEVELORDER valueset.

ORA-37551.m: The detail or leaf values of this hierarchy must all have the same level. This member is on a different level than most of the leaf values.

ORA-37552.m: DIMENSION_MEMBER must have an associated level that is found in the levelorder valueset so that we can figure out DIMENSION_MEMBER's level depth.

ORA-37553.m: A relation was used that had loops in it.

ORA-37554.m: A relation was used that contained loops.

ORA-38487.m: An attempt was made to use an un-approved function in the expression.

ORA-38488.m: An attempt was made to reassign an attribute set to an expression column.

ORA-38489.m: Predicate table creation failed due to the reported error.

ORA-38490.m: The quotes in the name did not match.

ORA-38491.m: Either the expression was not in a valid SQL-WHERE clause format or it had references to nonexistent schema objects.

ORA-38492.m: An invalid parameters clause was specified with the ALTER INDEX command.

ORA-38493.m: An attempt was made to create an Expression Filter index in Standard Edition.

ORA-38494.m: One of the attributes in the set has the same name as the name of one of the columns in the table configured for table alias.

ORA-38495.m: The actual data type for the stored attribute configured for the Expression Filter index object did not match the data type recorded in the Expression Filter dictionary.

ORA-38496.m: An attempt was made to REBUILD an Expression Filter index that was not valid.

ORA-38497.m: Index with a matching name does not exist or the index was not created using ExpFilter indextype.

ORA-38498.m: The expression filter index object has a stored or indexed attribute that had broken dependencies.

ORA-38499.m: The expression set already had a list of stored and indexed attributes. Additional attributes cannot be specified in the CREATE INDEX parameters clause.

ORA-38501.m: An attempt was made to use a sub-query in the expression.

ORA-38502.m: An attempt was made to use an invalid XML tag for the index.

ORA-38503.m: An attempt was made to modify the index parameters after the index creation.

ORA-38504.m: An attempt was made to use the operator binding with an attribute set containing more than one (table alias) attribute. This is not permitted.

ORA-38505.m: An attempt was made to use an invalid default value or a default that is larger than 100 characters.

ORA-38506.m: An attempt was made to use an invalid type for an attribute configured for text predicates.

ORA-38507.m: An attempt was made to configure an attribute of spatial or text data type as stored predicate group.

ORA-38601.m: The memory size did not satisfy the minimum memory requirement.

ORA-38602.m: The input cursor did not return exactly two columns for transactional input format or the input cursor didn't have consistent data types for horizontal input format

ORA-38603.m: The including & excluding cursor did not return exactly one column.

ORA-38604.m: The including & excluding cursor item-id type did not match input cursor item-id type Chapter 89 89-23

ORA-38605.m: There was insufficient available memory for candidate generation.

ORA-38606.m: The user inputed a support threshold not in the range of [0, 1].

ORA-38607.m: The inputed minimum or maximum itemset length exceed the internal maximum itemset length or less than 1.

ORA-38608.m: The user inputed minimum length is more than maximum length.

ORA-38609.m: The memory size did not satisfy the minimum memory requirement for tree counting.

ORA-38610.m: An error occurred because DBMS_FREQUENT_ITEMSET and prefix ORA_FI are reserved for the DBMS_FREQUENT_ITEMSET package's internal use.

ORA-38611.m: The input cursor's item type is not number or character type

ORA-38612.m: The item's length was more than half of one database block.

ORA-38613.m: Cursor parameter is returning less than 2 columns. Chapter 89 89-24

ORA-38620.m: Expressions in input cursor do not have an alias name.

ORA-38621.m: The user specified a max tree depth not in the range of [2, 20].

ORA-38622.m: The memory size did not satisfy the minimum memory requirement for decision tree building.

ORA-38700.m: The maximum number of flashback database log files was exceeded.

ORA-38701.m: This message reports the filename for details of another message.

ORA-38702.m: Could not write to the flashback database log file.

ORA-38703.m: A corrupt flashback database log file header was read.

ORA-38704.m: The flashback database log file header contained a checksum that does not match the value calculated from the file header as read from disk. This means the file header was corrupt.

ORA-38705.m: When the flashback log file header was read, the block size in the control file did not match the block size contained in the header. Chapter 89 89-25

ORA-38706.m: An ALTER DATABASE FLASHBACK ON command failed. Other messages in the alert log describe the problem.

ORA-38707.m: An ALTER DATABASE FLASHBACK ON command failed because media recovery was not enabled.

ORA-38708.m: An ALTER DATABASE FLASHBACK ON command failed because there was not enough space in the Recovery Area for the first flashback database log file.

ORA-38709.m: An ALTER DATABASE FLASHBACK ON command failed because the Recovery Area was not enabled.

ORA-38710.m: The flashback database log file was rejected because it appeared to be written by an incompatible version of Oracle.

ORA-38711.m: A corrupt Flashback Database log file block header was read. More information was dumped to the trace file.

ORA-38712.m: A corrupt flashback database log record header was read. Either the record type or length were incorrect. More information was dumped to the trace file.

ORA-38713.m: An ALTER DATABASE FLASHBACK ON command had no effect because flashback database logging was already on.

ORA-38714.m: An ALTER DATABASE FLASHBACK ON command failed because the database either crashed or was shutdown with the ABORT option.

ORA-38715.m: An invalid log number was used when dumping a flashback database log file.

ORA-38716.m: An invalid value was specified for TYPE in the DUMP FLASHBACK command.

ORA-38717.m: An invalid option was specified in the DUMP FLASHBACK command. Currently the only options allowed are: DBA, TYPE, and LOGICAL.

ORA-38718.m: An invalid thread number was used in dumping the flashback database log files for a thread.

ORA-38719.m: An invalid object was specified in a DUMP FLASHBACK command. Currently the only objects allowed are: LOGFILE or THREAD.

ORA-38720.m: A log file name or number was missing in a DUMP FLASHBACK LOGFILE command.

ORA-38721.m: An invalid file number was specified in the DBA clause of a DUMP FLASHBACK command.

ORA-38722.m: The ALTER DATABASE FLASHBACK command was specified without the ON or OFF keyword. Chapter 89 89-27

ORA-38723.m: The SCN keyword was specified in a FLASHBACK DATABASE command but the SCN expression was invalid.

ORA-38724.m: An invalid option was specified to the FLASHBACK DATABASE command. Valid options are: SCN or TIMESTAMP.

ORA-38725.m: The database name specified in a FLASHBACK DATABASE command did not match the name of the currently mounted database.

ORA-38726.m: A FLASHBACK DATABASE command was tried but flashback database logging has not been enabled.

ORA-38727.m: The control file being used is a backup control file.

ORA-38728.m: An SCN or time stamp provided in a FLASHBACK DATABASE command was in the future.

ORA-38729.m: There was not enough flashback database log data to do the FLASHBACK DATABASE.

ORA-38730.m: The expression supplied in a FLASHBACK DATABASE command was invalid.

ORA-38731.m: The version of the flashback database log file header was corrupt.

ORA-38732.m: The file size indicated in the control file did not match the file size contained in the flashback log file header. The flashback database log file was corrupt.

ORA-38733.m: A flashback database log file shrank in size. This was likely to have been caused by operator or operating system error.

ORA-38734.m: The database ID in the flashback database log file did not match the database ID in the control file.

ORA-38735.m: The log file number in the flashback database log file did not match the control file.

ORA-38736.m: The thread number in the flashback database log file did not match the control file.

ORA-38737.m: The flashback database log is corrupted or is an old version.

ORA-38738.m: A check of flashback database log file header at database open found that the flashback database log appeared to be an incorrectly restored backup. Flashback database log files cannot be backed up and restored. Chapter 89 89-29

ORA-38739.m: The control file change sequence number in the flashback database log file was greater than the number in the control file.

ORA-38740.m: A flashback database log file header contained a usable blocks value greater than the file size. The flashback database log file file is corrupt.

ORA-38741.m: The formatted blocks value in the flashback database log file was greater than the file size. The flashback database log file was corrupt.

ORA-38742.m: The flashback database log file header had log reset data that was different than the control file. The log was probably an incorrectly restored backup. Flashback database logs cannot be backed up.

ORA-38743.m: The Time/SCN provided in a FLASHBACK DATABASE command was in the future of the database.

ORA-38744.m: A different copy of the file was accessed the last time FLASHBACK DATABASE looked at the file header. A backup of the file was restored or the meaning of the file name changed during FLASHBACK DATABASE.

ORA-38746.m: An error occurred during a FLASHBACK DATABASE command. See other errors on error stack. Chapter 89 89-30

ORA-38747.m: A corrupt data block before image was encountered during a FLASHBACK DATABASE command.

ORA-38748.m: An attempt to do FLASHBACK DATABASE found that the file was not available for recovery. Either it was online and the database was open in some instance, or another process was currently doing media recovery or flashback on the file.

ORA-38749.m: An attempt was made to start a FLASHBACK DATABASE in the same session as a media recovery.

ORA-38750.m: An attempt was made to use a dispatcher process for FLASHBACK DATABASE. Memory requirements disallow this recovery method.

ORA-38751.m: This message reports the filename for details of another message.

ORA-38752.m: During an RMAN FLASHBACK DATABASE conversation, a file was listed which was not part of the database. The RMAN conversation was aborted.

ORA-38753.m: An attempt to perform a FLASHBACK DATABASE failed because the file does not have enough flashback log data to cover the time to flash back. Either the file did not have flashback generation enabled for it, or had flashback generation turned off for it some time during the time span of the flashback.

ORA-38754.m: A FLASHBACK DATABASE command did not start. A redo log needed for the recovery part of FLASHBACK DATABASE could not be found or accessed.

ORA-38755.m: An attempt was made to turn on flashback database logging for a tablespace that already has flashback turned on.

ORA-38756.m: An attempt was made to turn off flashback database logging for a tablespace that already has flashback off.

ORA-38757.m: An attempt to Flashback a database was made when the database was not mounted or was already open.

ORA-38758.m: An attempt to do FLASHBACK DATABASE found that the file was restored since the last standby recovery. The file cannot be flashed back.

ORA-38759.m: An attempt to turn on or off Flashback Database logging was made when the database was open or mounted by more than one instance.

ORA-38760.m: Database flashback is on but this instance failed to start generating flashback data. Look in alert log for more specific errors.

ORA-38761.m: A redo log needed for the recovery portion of FLASHBACK could not be read or opened. The FLASHBACK operation failed.

ORA-38762.m: The FLASHBACK DATABASE operation failed. The recovery portion of FLASHBACK DATABASE needed redo from the first SCN to the second, but one or more of the redo logs in the range could not be read or opened.

ORA-38763.m: A FLASHBACK DATABASE command did not start. The set of enabled threads changed during the time to flash back. Chapter 89 89-32

ORA-38764.m: A FLASHBACK DATABASE command did not start. The datafile was restored from a backup taken when the enabled threads were different than at the time of the flashback.

ORA-38765.m: A complete recovery was performed after a database flashback but the database was not opened for read-write access, or a FLASHBACK DATABASE command failed.

ORA-38766.m: An attempt to do FLASHBACK DATABASE found that the file was shrunk during the time to flash back.

ORA-38767.m: The value of parameters DB_FLASHBACK_RETENTION_TARGET must be same in all instances. All databases must have same flashback retention target parameters.

ORA-38768.m: An operating system error occurred when Flashback Database tried to shrink (resize) a datafile. Flashback shrinks a file in order to undo the effects of a file expand, for example, an autoextension of the file.

ORA-38769.m: A FLASHBACK DATABASE command failed after modifying the the database. Look in the alert log for more information about the failure.

ORA-38770.m: A FLASHBACK DATABASE command successfully restored the database but failed during the recovery step. Look in the alert log for more information about the failure.

ORA-38771.m: The recovery step of FLASHBACK DATABASE encountered the creation of a datafile that could not be added to the control file.

ORA-38772.m: The recovery step of FLASHBACK DATABASE encountered the creation of a datafile and could not recreate the file. The error from the creation attempt is displayed in another message. The control file file entry for the file is 'UNNAMEDnnnnn'.

ORA-38773.m: The recovery step of FLASHBACK database encountered the creation of a datafile and could not create the file because the file name is already in use in the database. The control file file entry for the file is 'UNNAMEDnnnnn'.

ORA-38774.m: An attempt was made to disable media recovery while flashback database was enabled.

ORA-38775.m: An attempt was made to set DB_RECOVERY_FILE_DEST to null while flashback database was enabled. Flashback database requires DB_RECOVERY_FILE_DEST to be set.

ORA-38776.m: During a database mount, the RVWR process discovered that the recovery area was disabled. DB_RECOVERY_FILE_DEST must have been set null or removed from the INIT.ORA file while the database was unmounted.

ORA-38777.m: A command was attempted that required the database to be mounted in this instance and not started in any other instance. ALTER DATABASE OPEN RESETLOGS requires that the database be started in only one instance if flashback database logging is enabled.

ORA-38778.m: The restore point name of the CREATE RESTORE POINT command already exists. A restore point name must be unique.

ORA-38779.m: The maximum number of restore points already have been created.

ORA-38780.m: The restore point name specified in the command did not exist.

ORA-38781.m: An attempt was made to disable media recovery while there is at least one guaranteed restore point.

ORA-38782.m: An attempt was made to flashback database to a non-guaranteed restore point while flashback database is off. You can only flashback a database to guaranteed restore point when flashback database is not on.

ORA-38783.m: An attempt was made to create a restore point when the database is in mount mode but it was not shutdown cleanly before it was mounted. In order to create a restore point when the database is mounted, the database must be shutdown cleanly before it is mounted.

ORA-38784.m: An attempt to create a restore point failed. See other errors on the error stack and in the alert log for the specific reason.

ORA-38785.m: Media recovery is not enabled. Media recovery must be enabled in order to create a guaranteed restore point. Chapter 89 89-35

ORA-38786.m: An attempt was made to perform a command that requires Flash recovery area to be enabled.

ORA-38787.m: While flashback database is not on, an attempt was made to create the first guaranteed restore point while the database is open.

ORA-38788.m: An attempt was made to create a restore point or a guaranteed while a physical standby database is not cleanly checkpointed.

ORA-38789.m: The file name provided in the command was longer than the maximum allowed.

ORA-38790.m: The FLASHBACK DATABASE command included the RESETLOGS parameter but not the BEFORE parameter.

ORA-38791.m: Flashback could not be started because a file was checkpointed or fuzzy at a point where the file can neither be restored nor recovered to our restore target.

ORA-38792.m: A Flashback Database logfile contains a record written by a future Oracle release and is unknown by this release.

ORA-38793.m: The Flashback Pluggable Database target SCN or timestamp is greater than the current database or pluggable database SCN/timestamp and the database or pluggable database incarnation is not the last opened incarnation.

ORA-38794.m: The Flashback Database target timestamp is not in the database's current incarnation or any of its ancestors.

ORA-38795.m: FLASHBACK DATABASE succeeded. However, if ALTER DATABASE OPEN RESETLOGS command was attempted now, it would fail with the specified error.

ORA-38796.m: There was not enough flashback log data to undo the flashback so a flashback was not started.

ORA-38797.m: An attempt was made to recover some datafiles or tablespaces of a database after the database had been flashed back. In order to recover control file and all datafiles correctly, full database recovery is required after a database has been flashed back.

ORA-38798.m: See other other messages on error stack for the cause.

ORA-38799.m: An attempt is made to drop the guaranteed restore point Oracle created internally for a snapshot standby. Chapter 89 89-37

ORA-38800.m: An attempt was made to start Redo Apply on an open physical standby database instance.

ORA-38801.m: An improper value was used for ORA_EDITION when trying to specify an edition to use at login time. The value is not understandable as an identifier, or is too long.

ORA-38802.m: This error occurred because a reference was made to an edition that does not exist or that the current user does not have privileges on.

ORA-38803.m: This error occurred because a reference was made to an unusable edition.

ORA-38804.m: This error occurred because an attempt was made to create an edition called ORA$BASE, DATABASE_DEFAULT, or USER_DEFAULT. These are not legal edition names. Or a value provided as an edition name is not understandable as an identifier, or is too long.

ORA-38805.m: An attempt was made to ALTER or DROP an edition that was being used in another session or was the database default edition.

ORA-38806.m: An attempt was made to use an edition that was in the process of being altered or dropped.

ORA-38807.m: This error occurred because an attempt was made to create more than one child edition for a parent edition.

ORA-38808.m: An attempt was made to make an edition with a usable parent and a usable child edition unusable.

ORA-38809.m: This error occurred because an attempt was made to drop a usable edition.

ORA-38810.m: This error occurred because an attempt was made to drop an edition that has a parent and a child edition.

ORA-38811.m: This error occurred because an attempt was made to drop an edition that has actual objects.

ORA-38812.m: The number of editions has reached the limit.

ORA-38813.m: The Oracle-supplied schema did not support editions.

ORA-38814.m: An attempt was made to execute an edition switch within an open transaction.

ORA-38815.m: An edition switch failed because it was executed from a PL/SQL block or using recursive SQL.

ORA-38816.m: This error occurred because an attempt was made to drop an edition that a child that inherits objects from the edition. Chapter 89 89-39

ORA-38817.m: You did not have sufficient privileges to execute the subprogram package DBMS_EDITIONS_UTIL.

ORA-38818.m: An attempt was made to violate the rule "A noneditioned object may not depend on an editioned object."

ORA-38819.m: An attempt was made to violate the rule "A noneditioned object may not depend on an editioned object."

ORA-38820.m: User could not be edition enabled if it has evolved object type.

ORA-38821.m: Could not bind or define ADT, VARRAY, nested table or REF data type when the specified Edition is different from the current edition.

ORA-38822.m: An attempt was made to create a referential integrity constraint to or from an editioned view or enable editions for a user that has views with referential integrity constraints.

ORA-38823.m: An attempt was made to drop the root edition when enabled crossedition trigger exists.

ORA-38824.m: An attempt was made to replace an existing object with a different value for the EDITIONABLE property.

ORA-38825.m: An attempt was made to alter the EDITIONABLE property of an object whose type has been enabled for editioning in the owner's schema.

ORA-38826.m: An attempt was made to create a TYPE or PACKAGE specification or body whose EDITIONABLE property did not match its counterpart.

ORA-38827.m: An edition switch failed because an attempt was made to change pluggable database (PDB) before the edition switch took effect.

ORA-38828.m: A change of the session-level edition failed because the overriding USE_DEFAULT_EDITION_ALWAYS mode was on.

ORA-38829.m: The FLASHBACK DATABASE command failed before modifying the the database. Look in the alert log for more information about the failure.

ORA-38850.m: A CREATE CONTROLFILE statement was given that did not list all the enabled threads for the database.

ORA-38851.m: The standby switchover or failover operation failed because it needs to mark the current instance (redo thread) as disabled.

ORA-38852.m: The open resetlogs or standby activation operation failed because it must use a different instance (redo thread) than the current instance (redo thread) to open the database.

ORA-38853.m: The standby switchover or failover operation failed because it needs to mark an instance (redo thread) as disabled. That instance was up, which prevented it from being disabled.

ORA-38854.m: The open resetlogs or standby activation operation failed because it needs to mark an instance (redo thread) as disabled. That instance was up, which prevented it from being disabled.

ORA-38855.m: The standby switchover or failover operation failed because it needs to mark an instance (redo thread) as enabled. However, it had less than 2 online redo logs, which prevented it from being enabled.

ORA-38856.m: The open resetlogs or standby activation operation failed because it needs to mark an instance (redo thread) as enabled. However, it had less than 2 online redo logs, which prevented it from being enabled.

ORA-38857.m: The standby switchover or failover operation failed because it needs to mark a redo thread as enabled. However, the control file was recreated with a MAXINSTANCES value smaller than the thread number of the redo thread.

ORA-38858.m: The open resetlogs or standby activation operation failed because it needs to mark a redo thread as enabled. However, the control file was recreated with a MAXINSTANCES value smaller than the thread number of the redo thread.

ORA-38859.m: The command attempted to switch the instance (thread) into a new log before disabling it. The switch attempt failed because all eligible online logs were either being cleared or not completely archived yet.

ORA-38860.m: The command was not permitted because the controlfile indicates the database was in the process of becoming a logical standby database but the controlfile conversion had not completed. Chapter 89 89-42

ORA-38861.m: Flashback recovery on the standby ended early because the user attempted to flashback to an SCN or time for which there were no redo logs.

ORA-38862.m: The command could not be performed because the flashback database logs were locked by another operation, such as FLASHBACK DATABASE or a query of V$FLASHBACK_DATABASE_LOG.

ORA-38863.m: Flashback Database could not flash back the file because it was restored from an RMAN optimized backup with deallocated objects. Flashback cannot revert the file to a time before the last deallocation in the datafile backup.

ORA-38864.m: An SCN or timestamp was specified when creating a guaranteed restore point. This is not supported. You can only create a guaranteed restore point as of the current time.

ORA-38865.m: An attempt was made to create a restore point with an SCN or timestamp that is in the future.

ORA-38866.m: An attempt was made to create a restore point with an SCN or timestamp that is before the beginning of the current database incarnation.

ORA-38867.m: An attempt was made to create a restore point with the specified timestamp while the database was not open. The database must be open in order to map the timestamp to an SCN.

ORA-38868.m: This control file was created or converted based on a control file from a time different from the time of the database.

ORA-38869.m: Flashback Database could not flash back the database because a file was restored from an RMAN backup with undo block compression, and the Flashback target was to a time when the missing undo may be needed to open the database.

ORA-38870.m: This control file was created or converted based on a control file from a time different from the time of the database.

ORA-38871.m: To enable flashback on a primary database, the database must be clean without requiring open resetlogs.

ORA-38872.m: RECOVER DATABASE with the BACKUP CONTROLFILE option, or a database recovery that required the control file be made a backup failed because Flashback Database was enabled or there was a guaranteed restore point.

ORA-38873.m: Flashback Database failed because incarnation information was not found in the control file. The Flashback target incarnation was not in the control file or had a missing parent incarnation.

ORA-38874.m: Flashback Database failed because incarnation information for the start of the offline range for a file was not found in the control file. Flashback could not update the file's header with the missing incarnation's resetlogs information.

ORA-38875.m: The FLASHBACK DATABASE command was not performed because it was started in test mode.

ORA-38876.m: The FLASHBACK DATABASE command was performed in test mode and determined that no redo logs were required to perform the tested FLASHBACK DATABASE command.

ORA-38877.m: The FLASHBACK DATABASE command was performed in test mode and determined that no redo logs required to perform the tested FLASHBACK DATABASE command were missing.

ORA-38878.m: A redo log needed for the recovery part of FLASHBACK DATABASE could not be found or accessed.

ORA-38879.m: A data file was plugged in when there was a guaranteed restore point, but Flashback Database was not enabled. It is possible that some changes to the data file, after it was converted, were not logged to the Flashback Database logs.

ORA-38880.m: Flashback database cannot undo the advance of database compatibility. Therefore, one cannot advance the compatibility of the database while there are guaranteed restore points in the database.

ORA-38881.m: An attempt was made to drop a tablespace on a primary database while there are guaranteed restore points. You cannot do this because Flashback database cannot undo dropping of a tablespace.

ORA-38882.m: An attempt was made to drop a tablespace on a standby database while there are guaranteed restore points. You cannot do this because Flashback database cannot undo dropping of a tablespace.

ORA-38883.m: An attempt was made to shrink a data file on a primary database while there are guaranteed restore points. You cannot do this because Flashback database cannot undo the shrinking of a data file.

ORA-38884.m: An attempt was made to shrink a data file on a standby database while there are guaranteed restore points. You cannot do this because Flashback database cannot undo the shrinking of a data file.

ORA-38885.m: Flashback database was disabled while fast-start failover was enabled.

ORA-38886.m: Recovery writer (RVWR) encountered an error when writing flashback database logs.

ORA-38887.m: An error occurred while writing to the flashback database logs.

ORA-38888.m: The provided thread number was invalid.

ORA-38889.m: An error occurred while trying to allocate PGA memory to store before images to write into flashback database log.

ORA-38890.m: Pluggable database (PDB) flashback or point-in-time recovery was performed on this PDB.

ORA-38891.m: There are two possible cause for this error: 1) The DB_FLASHBACK_LOG_DEST parameter was in use when no DB_FLASHBACK_LOG_DEST_SIZE parameter was encountered while fetching initialization parameter.

ORA-38892.m: An attempt was made to set DB_FLASHBACK_LOG_DEST to null while flashback database was enabled. Flashback database requires DB_FLASHBACK_LOG_DEST to be set.

ORA-38893.m: Parameter DB_FLASHBACK_LOG_DEST_SIZE specified was not valid.

ORA-38894.m: The value of parameters DB_FLASHBACK_LOG_DEST and DB_FLASHBACK_LOG_DEST_SIZE must be same in all instances. All databases must have same flashback destination parameters.

ORA-38895.m: There are two possible cause for this error: 1) The flashback log can only be either in DB_RECOVERY_FILE_DEST or DB_FLASHBACK_LOG_DEST but not both.

ORA-38896.m: Flashback logs can only be in either DB_RECOVERY_FILE_DEST or DB_FLASHBACK_LOG_DEST but not both. Because flashback database is already enabled Chapter 89 89-47 with flashback logs in DB_RECOVERY_FILE_DEST, one cannot specify DB_FLASHBACK_LOG_DEST in the INIT.ORA file.

ORA-38897.m: Flashback logs can only be in either DB_RECOVERY_FILE_DEST or DB_FLASHBACK_LOG_DEST but not both. Because flashback database is already enabled with flashback logs in DB_FLASHBACK_LOG_DEST, one cannot remove DB_FLASHBACK_LOG_DEST in the INIT.ORA file.

ORA-38898.m: DB_FLASHBACK_LOG_DEST_SIZE cannot be set to new value as it is lower than the minimum quota required.

ORA-38899.m: The use of DB_FLASHBACK_LOG_DEST requires Oracle Database 23c compatibility but currently the compatible initialization parameter was not set to release 23.0.0.0.0 or later version.

ORA-38900.m: Mandatory column of error logging table is not present

ORA-38901.m: Mandatory information column of error logging table is present, but must be at the beginning of the row.

ORA-38902.m: The operation failed because the array DML had more errors than can be stored internally for BATCH ERRORs.

ORA-38903.m: A DML Error Logging operation was attempted on a table which has an ADT, REF, VARRAY, or nested table column type, and the error logging table referred to the specified column. Chapter 89 89-48

ORA-38904.m: A DML Error Logging operation was attempted on a table which has a CLOB, NCLOB, or BLOB column type, and the error logging table referred to the specified column.

ORA-38905.m: A DML Error Logging operation was attempted on a table which has a LONG, or LONG RAW column type, and the error logging table referred to the specified column.

ORA-38909.m: A DML Error Logging operation was attempted on a table which has BATCH ERROR mode also specified.
