ORA-65400.m: User attempts to modify clustering clasue on a table that that does not have one.

ORA-65402.m: Invalid option on CLUSTERING clause. Only [YES | NO ] ON LOAD or [YES | NO ] ON DATA MOVEMENT are valid.

ORA-65403.m: Invalid usage of CLUSTERING clause. The clause can be specified only for [CREATE | ALTER] [TABLE | SNAPSHOT | MATERIALIZED VIEW]

ORA-65404.m: The CLUSTERING clause was specified multiple times for the table or the materialized view yet a table or materialized view can only be clustered by a single order.

ORA-65405.m: The CLUSTERING clause conflicted with other clauses on the table. The CLUSTERING clause cannot be used with IOT, CLUSTERED, GLOBAL TEMPORARY TABLE, or the EXTERNAL TABLE clause.

ORA-65406.m: The CLUSTERING clause was not followed by the BY [LINEAR | INTERLEAVED] ORDER option. Chapter 99 99-134

ORA-65407.m: The ADD CLUSTERING clause was specified on a table with an existing CLUSTERING clause.

ORA-65408.m: More than 10 columns were specified in the BY ORDER subclause. You can have at most 10 columns in the BY ORDER subclause.

ORA-65409.m: More than 40 columns or more than 4 groups were specified in the BY INTERLEAVED ORDER subclause. You can have at most 40 columns and at most 4 groups in the INTERLEAVED ORDER subclause.

ORA-65410.m: A non-scalar column was specified in the BY [LINEAR | INTERLEAVED] ORDER subclause. Also, virtual or hidden columns are not allowed.

ORA-65411.m: An attempt was made to modify or drop a CLUSTERING clause in a table that had no CLUSTERING clause associated with it.

ORA-65412.m: You specified an invalid option on ALTER TABLE...CLUSTERING. For example, you specified DROP and ADD CLUSTERING in one statement.

ORA-65413.m: An object was referenced in the CLUSTERING clause that could not be resolved to a base table reference.

ORA-65414.m: A column resolved to multiple tables in the CLUSTERING clause.

ORA-65415.m: The CLUSTERING clause had an OR condition and the individual join condition on the base table columns was not an equijoin. Chapter 99 99-135

ORA-65416.m: Joins in the CLUSTERING clause referenced a dimension table more than once.

ORA-65417.m: The dimension table in the CLUSTERING clause was illegal. Dimension table cannot be GLOBAL TEMPORARY TABLE or EXTERNAL TABLE.

ORA-65418.m: The join between the fact and dimension tables in the CLUSTERING clause did not have a primary or unique key constraint on the dimension table join column(s).

ORA-65419.m: Columns in the clustering group did not come from the same table. For example: .. CLUSTERING BY ..( (t1.c1, t2.c2), (t2.c1, t2.c2) ). Clustering group (t1.c1, t2.c2) must contain references to only one table.

ORA-65420.m: The data type of columns in the join condition of CLUSTERING clause were not compatible. They must be of the same type.

ORA-65421.m: An ALTER TABLE MODIFY COLUMN command was issued on a column on which a CLUSTERING clause exists.

ORA-65422.m: An ALTER TABLE MODIFY CLUSTERING command was issued with the WITH MATERIALIZED ZONEMAP option but the table already had zonemap define on it.

ORA-65423.m: An ALTER TABLE MODIFY CLUSTERING command was issued with the WITHOUT MATERIALIZED ZONEMAP option but the table did not have zonemap associated with the CLUSTERING clause.

ORA-65424.m: More than 4 joins were specified in the CLUSTERING clause. You can have at most 4 joins to the dimension tables.

ORA-65425.m: An attempt was made to define clustering on a table stored in non-Oracle Exadata storage.

ORA-65426.m: Oracle cannot perform data clustering because an attempt was made to populate a clustering column with non-scalar data values, or a clustering column defaulted to non-scalar values was omitted.

ORA-65427.m: An attempt was made to use a system-generated or unused column in a join condition in the CLUSTERING clause.

ORA-65428.m: An attempt was made to create a pluggable database with temporary tablespace files in the object store.

ORA-65429.m: An attempt was made to clone a source pluggable database (PDB) stored in an object store using a database link.

ORA-65430.m: An attempt was made to unplug a pluggable database (PDB) stored in an object store using an invalid credential.

ORA-65431.m: An attempt was made to unplug a pluggable database (PDB) to object store without a credential.

ORA-65432.m: An internal error occurred when reading or writing to XML file in object store.

ORA-65433.m: An attempt to copy a source file failed for the block ranges specified in the error message. The failure could be due to inability to access the source file.

ORA-65434.m: An attempt was made to cancel the relocate of a pluggable database that was not in relocate unplugged state.

ORA-65435.m: An attempt was made to refresh a pluggable database beyond the end of life span of the current source pdb.

ORA-65436.m: An attempt was made to transition a pluggable database to follow a new lifespan that did not match the current state of the pluggable database's datafiles.

ORA-65437.m: Either the data file or life span table was corrupt.

ORA-65438.m: An attempt was made to refresh the pluggable database without specifying a system change number (SCN) or a time to refresh.

ORA-65439.m: general event for automatic clustering module

ORA-65440.m: An attempt was made to resume a PDB clone with mismatched clauses.

ORA-65451.m: An attempt was made to use advanced index compression on an unsupported storage type.

ORA-65452.m: An attempt was made to clone a pluggable database (PDB) that was open read/write to a location on the object store.

ORA-65455.m: An invalid family was used to specify a parameter value.

ORA-65456.m: The specified family could not be used in the current instance type.

ORA-65457.m: The specified family could ot be used in the current instance type.

ORA-65458.m: Too many characters were specified in the parameter qualifier in the initialization parameter file.

ORA-65459.m: A SID was used with FAMILY.

ORA-65460.m: An invalid family was specified for this instance in ORACLE_FAMILY.

ORA-65461.m: The CONTAINER clause was set to ALL in the ALTER SYSTEM SET statement for a non-PDB modifiable or non-inheritable PDB parameter.

ORA-65462.m: An internal error was encountered while trying to update dependent parameter value.

ORA-65466.m: The specified family contained an illegal character or the family specified was too long, which cannot occur in an SPFILE setting. Illegal characters include *,#"'=() and whitespace.

ORA-65470.m: Lost write was already in the requested state. If enable or disable lost write was specified, then lost write was already in the requested state.

ORA-65471.m: LOST WRITE PROTECTION could not be enabled for lost write tablespaces or temporary tablespaces.

ORA-65472.m: An invalid option was specified while altering a LOST WRITE PROTECTION tablespace.

ORA-65473.m: An invalid option was specified while creating or altering a flashback tablespace.

ORA-65474.m: Enabling lost write protection could not succeed because the lost write tablespaces did not exist or had insufficient space. It is possible that shadow lost write was not enabled for the database.

ORA-65476.m: There was an internal error code.

ORA-65477.m: The object that was manipulated could not be set to the requested state.

ORA-65478.m: A lost write was found in a data block protected by lost write.

ORA-65479.m: An attempt was made to perform an operation affecting a pluggable database while connected to a different pluggable database.

ORA-65480.m: An invalid option was specified while creating LOST WRITE PROTECTION tablespace.

ORA-65481.m: In CREATE TABLESPACE, the LOST WRITE PROTECTION option was specified more than once.

ORA-65482.m: An invalid option was specified while creating a tablespace.

ORA-65483.m: The BIGFILE keyword was not specified while creating a LOST WRITE PROTECTION tablespace.

ORA-65484.m: An attempt was made to assign a LOST WRITE PROTECTION tablespace to be a user's default tablespace.

ORA-65485.m: An attempt was made to create user objects in a LOST WRITE PROTECTION tablespace. Other system objects such as rollback segments are also illegal.

ORA-65486.m: An attempt was made to assign a LOST WRITE PROTECTION tablespace to be a default tablespace.

ORA-65487.m: A different block size was specified when a SHADOW LOST WRITE tablespace was created.

ORA-65488.m: An attempt was made to enable SHADOW LOST WRITE on a standby database.

ORA-65489.m: An attempt was made to enable lost write when the database was not open or an access error occurred when attempting to access the new_lost_write_shadows$ dictionary table

ORA-65490.m: Invalid parameters were specified for a LOST WRITE PROTECTION tablespace. Only BIGFILE TABLESPACE, SIZE and BLOCKSIZE for a LOST WRITE PROTECTION tablespace can be specified upon creation. ALTER cannot be used to make a LOST WRITE PROTECTION tablespace read-only.

ORA-65491.m: The lost write tablespace does not exist.

ORA-65493.m: An attempt was made to shrink a lost write tablespace.

ORA-65494.m: There was an internal error in lost write subsystem. The SYSAUX tablespace may be inaccessible or full.

ORA-65495.m: There was an internal error in the lost write protection subsystem.

ORA-65496.m: One of the lost write tracking tables was inconsistent. A parallel operation may have caused this problem.

ORA-65498.m: An internal error occurred when performing drop side effects

ORA-65499.m: The attempted lost write operation is prohibited on a read-only database.

ORA-65500.m: DB_UNIQUE_NAME could not be modified, because a database resource identified by DB_UNIQUE_NAME existed in the cluster. To modify DB_UNIQUE_NAME, any resource it identifies must first be removed.

ORA-65501.m: In a pluggable database environment, an attempt was made to modify a LOB using a locator which belongs to a different container.

ORA-65503.m: An attempt was made to send or receive a temporary LOB locator over a database link.

ORA-65504.m: An attempt was made to send or receive a temporary LOB locator from an index-organized table over a database link.

ORA-65505.m: An attempt was made to pass a remote LOB locator as a parameter to a LOB operation which does not accept a remote LOB locator.

ORA-65506.m: An attempt was made to pass Large Object (LOB) locators belonging to a different database as a parameter to a Large Object (LOB) operation.

ORA-65507.m: An attempt was made to select a Large Objects (LOB) locator from a remote database that was obtained by the remote from another remote database.

ORA-65508.m: An attempt was made to send or receive an invalid LOB locator over a database link.

ORA-65509.m: An attempt was made to perform an operation INSERT AS SELECT on a view with INSTEAD OF trigger on it and a remote LOB column in the select list.

ORA-65510.m: An attempt was made to send or receive a LOB locator over a database link to a pre-12.2 database.

ORA-65511.m: This is likely the result of passing a local LOB locator as a bind value to a SQL statement that is executed on a remote database over a database link.

ORA-65512.m: When the pluggable database was closed, any temporary LOBs in it were freed. An attempt was made to access a freed LOB.

ORA-65513.m: The LOB locator operands that were passed to the SQL operator did not have the value-based property set uniformly.

ORA-65514.m: An invalid value was specified for LOB ENABLE STORAGE IN ROW. The specified value for LOB ENABLE STORAGE IN ROW should be null, 4000, 8000, 16000 or 32000 for SECUREFILE LOBS in heap tables, and null in all other cases.

ORA-65515.m: The inline size specified for the ENABLE STORAGE IN ROW clause of either the CREATE or ALTER TABLE was greater than the block size of the tablespace of the table.

ORA-65516.m: An inline size greater than 4000 bytes in the LOB storage clause was specified for a case other than the top level LOB column. Increased LOB inlining is not supported in LOB usage cases like abstract data types (ADTs), nested tables and varrays.

ORA-65531.m: The attempted operation resulted in the number of index levels exceeding the maximum allowed.

ORA-65532.m: An attempt was made to alter or drop an automatically created index. Chapter 99 99-145

ORA-65533.m: An alter index coalesce command could not fully complete. Further coalescing may still be possible.

ORA-65535.m: Oracle Database clients earlier than release 12.1 could not handle error codes larger than 65535.
